name: WDL On Slurm
summary: Run small WDL workflows inside Slurm allocations with miniwdl.
description: This skill provides a conservative pattern for running Workflow Description Language workflows with miniwdl inside a Slurm allocation, including output and run directory controls, a tiny word-count smoke test, input JSON handling, and dry-run-first launch records.

inputs.name: run_dir
inputs.description: Directory under which miniwdl creates the workflow run directory.

inputs.name: runtime_policy
inputs.description: Optional local policy for containers, runtime defaults, copy-input behavior, and scheduler allocation limits.

outputs.name: wdl_launch_plan
outputs.description: Reviewable miniwdl command, input paths, run directory, output JSON path, and Slurm allocation details.

outputs.name: wdl_outputs
outputs.description: miniwdl output JSON and run directory when RUN_WDL=1.

outputs.name: run_record
outputs.description: Log file with miniwdl version, command provenance, and selected runtime options.

examples.title: Plan or run a WDL smoke test in Slurm

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check the Slurm wrapper shell syntax.
Run the wrapper in default dry-run mode, then set RUN_WDL=1 on a short allocation after loading a site-approved miniwdl environment.
